prompt:
Cluster comprises IBM's Opteron-based eServer 325 server and systems management software and storage devices that can run Linux and Windows operating systems.
IBM on Tuesday announced a prepackaged and pretested cluster that is powered by Advanced Micro Devices Inc.s 64-bit Opteron processor.
IB
completion:
M said the Cluster comprises IBM BigFix cluster management software, the SelectBox blade server that runs on the SelectBox mid-range storage device and one XFS-licensed data storage array. All of the host nodes and storage in the cluster communicate using the XFS protocol, IBM said.
The server includes the OneX data-optimized storage.
The system management software uses an IBM-developed trace agent for monitoring management activities in the system.
The pretreated cluster will be available in first half of 2009.
Mark Hurd, president of the Storage Products Group at IBM, called the Cluster a proof of concept and said the company will provide detailed information on how to build a cluster and set up the testing.
(Reporting by Kevin Sharkey in San Francisco)

prompt:
Cluster comprises IBM's Opteron-based eServer 325 server and systems management software and storage devices that can run Linux and Windows operating systems.
IBM on Tuesday announced a prepackaged and pretested cluster that is powered by Advanced Micro Devices Inc.s 64-bit Opteron processor.
IB
completion:
M and Advanced Micro Devices both plan to sell the hardware, or pre-packaged Linux operating systems and servers.
Companies in the cloud computing service space - such as Amazon.com Inc - are looking at new ways of delivering computing capabilities.
This strategy focuses on enabling customers to deploy enterprise applications more rapidly and easily without capital investments.
The Advanced Micro Devices Opteron 64-bit processor is a 512-core server chip that is packed with special technologies, IBM said in a statement on Tuesday.
The processor is suitable for companies that want standard storage, but have also seen an increase in demand to enable the continued growth of services, IBM said.
The customers do not usually need to know how to design a particular piece of the puzzle, which is why IBM developed this premade cluster module.
The Hard Cloud Enterprise group helps develop prebuilt, discounted server packages and offers integration of software, IBM said in a statement on Tuesday.

prompt:
Belying expectations, Prasar Bharti has earned only Rs 58.19 crore (Rs 581.9 million) as revenue during the Commonwealth Games last month.
The gross revenue earned by PB on account of telecasting/broadcasting of advertisements on Doordarshan channel and All India Radio during coverage of
completion:
the CWG was Rs 31.85 crore. However, this was reduced to Rs 27.51 crore (Rs 290.5 million) by way of tax rebate.
The revenue earned by PB on account of advertisement sales on Doordarshan channel was Rs 11.29 crore. Meanwhile, on the back of IPL and the Commonwealth Games, PB earned Rs 106 crore during the April-October period. The revenue earned was mainly driven by the ad sale of the Doordarshan channel.
Following the IT Act, PB has been required to increase its overall IT budget by 20 percent from Rs 636 crore in 2018-19 to Rs 856 crore in 2019-20.
Through advertisement sales, PB has been raising its ad income by 10-12 percent every year.

prompt:
Belying expectations, Prasar Bharti has earned only Rs 58.19 crore (Rs 581.9 million) as revenue during the Commonwealth Games last month.
The gross revenue earned by PB on account of telecasting/broadcasting of advertisements on Doordarshan channel and All India Radio during coverage of
completion:
CWG-2018 was Rs 2.58 crore (Rs 26.3 crore). This included advertising of association (IPTV) fees which was Rs 986.6 crore (Rs 14.6 crore).
On account of PTI Sports India Limited fee earned by the national sports entities, Prasar Bharti got only Rs 21.57 crore (Rs 112.8 crore). With Rs 69.79 crore (Rs 15.5 crore) income from All India Radio, it is only Rs 12 crore (Rs 25.3 crore).
State governments’ fee earned by the national sports entities was Rs 421.4 crore (Rs 15.4 crore). Add to it the advertising of private companies which earned by Bharti Sports Corporation and Swadeshi Junior Corps, Prasar Bharti earned Rs 1,706 crore (Rs 21.5 crore).
The earning by association, franchise and all the IPTV distribution fee charges on national entities was Rs 2,148.

prompt:
Marketers are employed in the public sector as well as the private sector.
Most people think of marketing as a strictly private sector activity, but the reality is people with sales and marketing backgrounds are hired by government agencies in a number of capacities. Government agencies at the local, state and federal level all employ marketing professionals in
completion:
one way or another. The Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis employs more than 1,000 marketing professionals, according to its website.
The fact that people with marketing backgrounds work for public-sector entities does not mean that the people with marketing backgrounds are less than qualified. In fact, most of those folks are highly qualified, especially when it comes to sales and marketing. These folks can attract contracts from the state and county governments, the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis, Fortune 500 companies and Fortune 100 companies and have the ability to turn those contracts into lucrative contracts for themselves.
Get a jumpstart on the new year with Marianne’s free online marketing strategy guide.

The France-based International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC), part of the WHO, put processed meats on the top of their “
completion:
emerging” list of carcinogens.
IARC estimates that processed meat such as bacon, sausages and ham are linked to the risk of colon and lung cancer, but noted that animal-based meats such as eggs and chickens are also carcinogenic.
Consumption of processed meats is strongly linked to the rise in oral and anal cancer, IARC noted, due to the higher levels of cancers of the mouth and colon and rectum.
DEADLY MEDICATIONS
The IARC’s report is particularly worrying since processed meats are extremely common in the population, constituting 20 percent of the world’s diet in some countries, as “food of choice” for so many people.

prompt:
An eagerly awaited report from the World Health Organization (WHO) states that processed meats such as bacon and sausages cause cancer, and red meat likely does so too.
The France-based International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC), part of the WHO, put processed meats on the top of their “
completion:
possible human carcinogens” list. But this is only a recommendation, and manufacturers of processed meats will fight any restrictions the agency puts in place.
Simply speaking, processed meats cause diseases such as type 2 diabetes and coronary artery disease. Red meat has shown to increase heart disease risk by as much as one third, and WHO believes processed meats “may also produce adverse health outcomes associated with serious illness or death.”
Some of these heart risks include hypertension, stroke, and cancer. Experts want people to recognize the seriousness of processed meats, but many end users choose to ignore the warnings and downplay health risks. This doesn’t help, but the WHO believes eating processed meats can help prevent heart disease and cancer.
Will processed meats stop causing cancer, heart disease, and stroke? Clearly, the WHO would like us to think so. But there are other factors beyond processed meats that should be considered. It’s clear that processed meats can cause some health problems

prompt:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) - U.S. Treasury Secretary Jack Lew on Sunday warned Congress against manufacturing a crisis over federal spending in the months ahead, as looming deadlines set the stage for a repeat of the political deadlock which two years ago triggered worldwide financial market turmoil.
In coming negotiations with Republicans, who control the House
completion:
of Representatives and the Senate, Lew said President Barack Obama could not afford to start excessive spending measures in 2013 if he did not raise tax rates on the wealthy and big businesses.
BORDER SECURITY, FISCAL CONGRAVITY
Congress will wrest the fate of another $1.2 trillion of U.S. borrowing authority in April before another stopgap measure is set in May. Republicans want the Obama administration to raise income taxes on the wealthy, while Democrats say higher taxes would be too expensive.
“The U.S. is back to dealing with fiscal cliff because we don’t like what’s on the table and we don’t like what’s on the table alone,” Lew told a meeting of ministers from emerging economies in Washington.
“Both sides need to put aside their personal political agendas and do what is in the best interest of the U.S. economy,” Lew said.

prompt:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) - U.S. Treasury Secretary Jack Lew on Sunday warned Congress against manufacturing a crisis over federal spending in the months ahead, as looming deadlines set the stage for a repeat of the political deadlock which two years ago triggered worldwide financial market turmoil.
In coming negotiations with Republicans, who control the House
completion:
of Representatives, Lew said he remained “persuaded this recession will end faster than the recession after 9/11 and that deficit reduction actions that were vital in 2008 are still vitally needed.”
With congressional leaders struggling to bridge differences over spending levels and entitlement cuts, even with Republican Donald Trump still undecided on the nomination of conservative Ted Cruz to replace Barack Obama as president next year, it appeared unlikely they would manage to produce a deal before the 2016 presidential election, and at a time of more vulnerable Republican leadership.
Republicans are already criticizing the festering looming threat of automatic “sequestration” cuts to defense and social programs, saying they were overly optimistic when they boosted spending on the military following the U.S. invasion of Iraq.
“Rather than pleading with the Congress to avert another ‘sequestration holiday,’ the administration will continue to communicate its intention to take the steps necessary to lower deficits and reduce the national debt,”

completion:
, the low returns of developing markets combined with the dwindling investor appetite for safe haven assets – gold or Japan, for example – have led many investment managers to become increasingly selective. Two UAE orders for military helicopters and guided bombs capped a remarkable year for procurement in which the Emirates became the largest foreign purchaser of US defence equipment, a Pentagon agency said.
The UAE, which has peacekeepers in Afghanistan, awarded Sikorksy Aircraft a US$171 million (Dh628m) contract for 14
completion:
Sikorsky S-92B helicopters.
The Middle Eastern country also purchased 23 US 90-millimetre anti-tank guided missiles and 210 Flex Active Stand-Off Pod Air Defence Systems, said Adam Stump, a Pentagon spokesman.
The announcement caps a year of record for the US government's defence contracting programme.
The year also saw contracts worth US$336m awarded to Lockheed Martin for small patrol boats, Blackwater USA for a 160-ton naval transport ship and General Dynamics for missiles for 12 helicopters.
US-made security gear was also sold to allies in the Gulf.
The announcement comes less than two months after President Donald Trump lashed out at fellow Gulf allies Riyadh, Abu Dhabi, and Dubai for "being at war with each other" by signing a coalition agreement to combat Iran.
The move infuriated the Gulf countries and Trump described the deal as "one of the worst deals anywhere ever made."

TBM-35C pyralis helicopters and 1 KA-31D-2 engineering-test aircraft on 12 March, US defence agency, but said this was subject to modifications.

prompt:
Two UAE orders for military helicopters and guided bombs capped a remarkable year for procurement in which the Emirates became the largest foreign purchaser of US defence equipment, a Pentagon agency said.
The UAE, which has peacekeepers in Afghanistan, awarded Sikorksy Aircraft a US$171 million (Dh628m) contract for 14
completion:
attack helicopters and a rocket-guided bomb to replace its Bell 212 crew-chief car, Pentagon officials said on Friday.
The contracts for missile defence equipment were worth US$66 million for the Kwajalein-5G and intermediate range of more than 6,000 miles to replace missiles on F-16 fighter jets and US$90 million for precision air-to-air missiles, the officials said.
The contracts were made under an unclassified authority for the Pentagon which permits foreign buyers of defence equipment to transfer the technology needed to make the systems to them.
The officers quoted in Thursday’s release did not say who provided the technology, but the small state of Oman was the first Gulf country to take such a step.
Sikorsky provides aerial surveillance, engine-powered infrared vision and satellite-based monitoring to defense, law enforcement and civilian customers. Its aerial attack helicopter line includes the Black Hawk G3 and VIP model.
